provenance.source_corpus: Effects of Changes in Acidity on Aquatic Insects in Rocky Littoral Habitats of Lakes Near Sudbur y, Ontario

source_spans.text: Direct and indirect effects of lake acidity appeared to be major controls on the structure of these communities

nodes.name: abundances of several taxa of Ephemeroptera

source_spans.text: Declines in abundances of several taxa of Ephemeroptera at pH below 5.5 were attributable to acid toxicity
increases in the abundances of Odonata and Diptera at pH below 5.5 were associated with the absence of fish predators and other indirect effects of acidity
increases in the abundances of Odonata and Diptera at pH below 5.5 were associated with the absence of fish predators and other indirect effects of acidity
Neutralization led to recolonization or increased abundance of the acid-sensitive mayfly, Stenacron interpunctatum

nodes.name: abundance of the acid-sensitive mayfly, Stenacron interpunctatum

source_spans.text: Neutralization led to recolonization or increased abundance of the acid-sensitive mayfly, Stenacron interpunctatum

nodes.name: abundance of the dragonfly, Boyeria grafiana

source_spans.text: Neutralization led to recolonization or increased abundance of the acid-sensitive mayfly, Stenacron interpunctatum, and the dragonfly, Boyeria grafiana

declines in the abundances of the dragonflies Aeshna interrupta, Aeshna eremita, and Leucorrhinia glacialis in the neutralized lakes were associated with reintroductions of Salvelinus fontinalis (aurora trout) and increased fish predation pressure
